title: Watching the Stars
summary: A narrator lies on the grass in his backyard on a clear night and describes the experience of stargazing — what he sees, what he thinks about, and how the night sky makes him feel.
body: Last night was so clear and warm that I decided to lie down on the grass in my backyard and look at the stars. I turned off the lights in the house and waited for my eyes to adjust to the darkness. After a few minutes, I could see thousands of tiny white lights scattered across the sky. The Milky Way was visible as a pale band stretching from one side of the sky to the other. I had not seen it that clearly since I was a child. I lay there for a long time without moving, just watching. Occasionally a star seemed to flicker, and once I saw a shooting star streak across the sky and disappear. I made a wish, as you do. As I stared upward, I started thinking about how large the universe is. The stars I could see were so far away that the light leaving them took millions of years to reach my eyes. That thought made my head spin a little. I felt very small, but also surprisingly calm. There was something peaceful about lying in the quiet dark and realising that most of our daily worries are really quite small in the big picture. I stayed outside until I started to shiver. When I finally went inside, I felt different — a little quieter, a little lighter. I think everyone should look at the stars properly at least once in a while.

annotations.meaningVi: thích nghi với bóng tối (nói về mắt)
annotations.exampleEn: Give your eyes a minute to adjust to the darkness.

annotations.meaningVi: rải rác khắp, phân tán trên
annotations.exampleEn: Books were scattered across the floor.
On a dark night in the countryside, you can see the Milky Way.
A pale band of light stretched across the horizon.
The candle flickered in the wind.
I saw a shooting star and closed my eyes to make a wish.

annotations.meaningVi: như người ta vẫn làm (cụm từ hài hước, bình thường hóa một hành động)
annotations.exampleEn: He walked into the meeting with coffee in one hand and a cat in the other, as you do.

annotations.meaningVi: khiến đầu tôi quay cuồng, choáng ngợp
annotations.exampleEn: The maths problem made my head spin.

annotations.meaningVi: bình tĩnh một cách đáng ngạc nhiên
annotations.exampleEn: She was surprisingly calm before the big presentation.

annotations.meaningVi: trong bức tranh tổng thể, xét trên toàn cục
annotations.exampleEn: One bad day doesn't matter much in the big picture.
He started to shiver when the temperature dropped.

annotations.phrase: at least once in a while
annotations.exampleEn: You should visit your grandparents at least once in a while.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple with 'since' for the last time something happened
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had not seen it that clearly since I was a child' — đây là thì quá khứ hoàn thành phủ định với 'since'. Dùng 'had not + past participle + since' để nói về khoảng thời gian từ lần cuối cùng điều gì đó xảy ra. 'Since' ở đây nghĩa là 'kể từ khi'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had not seen it that clearly since I was a child.' Use 'had not + past participle + since' to say how long it has been since something last happened. 'Since' introduces the last time point. Compare: 'I haven't seen it since' (present) vs 'I had not seen it since' (past narrative).

grammarNotes.title: Gerunds after prepositions: 'without moving', 'about lying'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Sau giới từ (without, about, of, for, before...), ta luôn dùng V-ing (gerund), không dùng động từ nguyên thể. Ví dụ: 'I lay there without moving' (not 'without move'). 'Something peaceful about lying in the quiet dark.' Đây là quy tắc rất quan trọng trong tiếng Anh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: After prepositions (without, about, of, for, before, after...), always use a gerund (verb + -ing), not the bare infinitive. Examples: 'I lay without moving.' / 'Something peaceful about lying in the dark.' / 'I started thinking about how large the universe is.' This is a consistent grammar rule.

translationVi: Tối qua trời trong xanh và ấm áp đến mức tôi quyết định nằm trên cỏ ngoài sân sau và ngắm sao. Tôi tắt đèn trong nhà và chờ mắt thích nghi với bóng tối. Sau vài phút, tôi có thể thấy hàng nghìn ánh sáng trắng nhỏ rải rác khắp bầu trời. Dải Ngân Hà hiện ra như một dải màu nhạt trải dài từ bên này sang bên kia bầu trời. Tôi chưa thấy nó rõ như vậy kể từ khi còn nhỏ. Tôi nằm đó rất lâu mà không cử động, chỉ ngắm nhìn. Thỉnh thoảng một ngôi sao dường như nhấp nháy, và một lần tôi thấy một ngôi sao băng vạch qua bầu trời rồi biến mất. Tôi cầu một điều ước, như người ta vẫn làm. Trong khi nhìn lên, tôi bắt đầu nghĩ về sự rộng lớn của vũ trụ. Những ngôi sao tôi nhìn thấy ở xa đến mức ánh sáng từ chúng mất hàng triệu năm để đến mắt tôi. Suy nghĩ đó khiến đầu tôi quay cuồng một chút. Tôi cảm thấy rất nhỏ bé, nhưng cũng bình tĩnh một cách đáng ngạc nhiên. Có gì đó yên bình khi nằm trong bóng tối yên lặng và nhận ra rằng hầu hết những lo lắng hàng ngày của chúng ta thực ra rất nhỏ bé trong bức tranh tổng thể. Tôi ở ngoài cho đến khi bắt đầu run vì lạnh. Khi cuối cùng vào nhà, tôi cảm thấy khác đi — yên tĩnh hơn một chút, nhẹ nhõm hơn một chút. Tôi nghĩ mọi người nên ngắm sao một cách nghiêm túc ít nhất thỉnh thoảng.

questions.prompt: Why did the narrator decide to go outside and look at the stars?
options: He heard about a meteor shower on the news
The night was so clear and warm
His friend told him to try stargazing
questions.explanation: 'Last night was so clear and warm that I decided to lie down on the grass.'

questions.prompt: What did the narrator do before looking at the sky?
options: He turned off the lights in the house
He got a blanket and a drink
questions.explanation: He 'turned off the lights in the house and waited for my eyes to adjust.'
questions.hint: He did something practical to help him see the stars better.

questions.prompt: How is the Milky Way described in the text?
options: As a bright circle of stars
As a cluster of blue and white lights
As a pale band stretching across the sky
As a moving stream of light
questions.explanation: 'The Milky Way was visible as a pale band stretching from one side of the sky to the other.'
questions.hint: The shape and colour of the Milky Way are both described.

questions.prompt: When was the last time he had seen the Milky Way so clearly?
options: Last year on a camping trip
A few months ago in the countryside
questions.explanation: 'I had not seen it that clearly since I was a child.'
questions.hint: He refers to a much earlier period of his life.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator see streak across the sky?
questions.explanation: He 'saw a shooting star streak across the sky and disappear.'
questions.hint: It is a natural phenomenon associated with making wishes.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator do when he saw the shooting star?
options: He wrote it in a diary
questions.explanation: 'I made a wish, as you do.'
questions.hint: It is a tradition associated with shooting stars.

questions.prompt: What thought made his head spin?
options: That the stars might not be real
That he was lying outside alone
That the light from stars takes millions of years to reach us
That the universe might be getting smaller
questions.explanation: 'The stars I could see were so far away that the light leaving them took millions of years to reach my eyes.'
questions.hint: It is a fact about the age of light from distant stars.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel despite thinking about the vastness of space?
questions.explanation: He 'felt very small, but also surprisingly calm.'
questions.hint: His emotional state was unexpected given the overwhelming thought.

questions.prompt: What did he realise while lying in the dark?
options: That he needed to change his lifestyle
That the stars were closer than he thought
That most daily worries are small in the big picture
That he should spend more time outdoors
questions.explanation: 'Most of our daily worries are really quite small in the big picture.'
questions.hint: It is a reflection on perspective and the size of our problems.

questions.prompt: Why did the narrator finally go inside?
options: He started to shiver from the cold
questions.explanation: 'I stayed outside until I started to shiver.'
questions.hint: His body gave him a physical signal to go in.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator describe his feeling when he went inside?
options: A little quieter and a little lighter
questions.explanation: He 'felt different — a little quieter, a little lighter.'
questions.hint: Two positive emotional shifts are described.

blanks.explanation: 'backyard' — the area of grass behind a house.
'adjust' — 'adjust to the darkness' means for eyes to adapt so they can see in the dark.
'scattered' — scattered means spread randomly across an area.
'stretching' — the Milky Way was stretching (spreading) across the sky.
'child' — he had not seen it that clearly since he was a child.
'flicker' — to flicker means to shine unsteadily, like a candle in the wind.
'wish' — it is a tradition to make a wish when you see a shooting star.
'universe' — he thought about how large the universe is.
'spin' — 'made my head spin' means caused a dizzying or overwhelming feeling.
'picture' — 'in the big picture' means when you consider everything broadly.
'shiver' — to shiver means to shake because of cold; it told him it was time to go inside.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says that looking at the stars reminded him that our daily worries are 'quite small in the big picture.' Do you think regularly stepping back and thinking about the universe is a healthy habit, or can it make everyday life feel meaningless? Is there a balance between perspective and caring about the small things?
